verifier_issues.detail: Top-level grader fields include failed_criteria: ["ri_2"] while passed_criteria lists ["ri_1","ri_2","ri_3","ri_4"] (contradiction). Later sections of the grader output and rubric_item_scores also disagree about whether ri_2 passed (some entries show passed: true, others show passed: false). This internal inconsistency undermines confidence in which criteria were judged as passed.
verifier_issues.suggested_fix: Normalize the grader output so that passed_criteria/failed_criteria and rubric_item_scores are consistent. Explicitly mark ri_2 as passed:false (or true) in all summary fields and regenerate aggregated scores.
verifier_issues.title: Inconsistent rubric pass/fail metadata for criterion ri_2

verifier_issues.detail: Stdout preview contains the assertion 'The workbook is formula-based' but the artifact preview for attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] shows parse_status 'openpyxl_unavailable' with preview_error_message 'No module named \u0027openpyxl\u0027'. Because the workbook content could not be parsed, the claim that formulas are present in the workbook is not independently verifiable from the provided previews and thus is effectively unsupported by the available evidence.
evidence_refs: GraderResult.limitations (notes inability to inspect workbook formulas)
verifier_issues.suggested_fix: Provide an inspectable representation of workbook formulas (e.g., enable openpyxl preview, export a sheet with SHOWFORMULAS, include a CSV of formulas, or attach screenshots). Alternatively, mark the claim as 'asserted by worker' and treat it as unverified in scoring.
verifier_issues.title: Unsupported claim about workbook being formula-based

verifier_issues.detail: Deterministic precheck and artifact-contract checks yield score 1.0, while model-judged final_score is 0.82 (with model_score 0.82). The grader justifies lowering the score due to inability to verify live formulas (ri_2). This reasoning is plausible and consistent with available memo text, but the grader's internal inconsistency around ri_2 (see high-severity issue) and duplicated/conflicting rubric entries should be resolved to fully trust the numeric aggregation.
evidence_refs: GraderResult.model_note and rubric_item_scores (ri_2 given partial credit)
verifier_issues.suggested_fix: After fixing the rubric pass/fail inconsistency, re-run score aggregation and include an explicit, reproducible calculation showing how per-item scores (and item weights) produce the final_score.
verifier_issues.title: Score provenance and deterministic precheck vs. model score explained but should be clearer

No hidden-reference leakage detected.

If workbook formulas cannot be inspected, explicitly state in grader output which judgments rely solely on memo evidence and mark them as provisional.

verifier_issues_summary: Workbook formulas could not be programmatically verified in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable) - limitation is correctly documented by the grader.
Minor arithmetic inconsistency in the memo: the grader correctly flags a reported combined cost of 215,500 while the listed option costs sum to 195,500.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.notes: All grader claims are supported by the trace and artifact previews: artifacts are present, the memo preview contains the reproduced packet rows and the budget/costs, and the environment trace shows missing spreadsheet libraries (consistent with openpyxl_unavailable). The final semantic score (0.89) matches the weighted aggregation of per-criterion scores using the stated rubric weights. No hidden-reference leakage detected.
